Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: First I need to remember what each constraint means and go through each activity first right The activities are RO CA RI PI QC PO GSL D IC RP

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: First lets start with activ freq thats the occurrence counts per activity Lets think about each RO Every order starts with Receive Order right So each case order has exactly 1 RO

Name: Hypothesis Generation - IND
Text: CA Do we check availability every time Yeah unless maybe the order is invalid Wait no wait if you receive an order you have to check availability

Name: Backtracking - PE
Text: Wait maybe lets think Could there be a case where you dont check availability No wait no

Name: Hypothesis Generation - IND
Text: RI Reserve items once you check availability you reserve them right So exactly 1 per order that proceeds past CA Wait no if availability check fails do you reserve No

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: So RI can be 0 or 1 PI Pick items if you reserved you pick So again 0 or 1

Name: Hypothesis Generation - IND
Text: QC Quality check do you do that every time you pick Or could you skip

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: PO GSL D IC RP all look like 0 or 1 depending on whether the order proceeds

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: RO is definitely 1 because every case is an order thats received

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Now next equivalence constraints Equivalence means if A occurs then B occurs the same number of times

Name: Hypothesis Generation - IND
Text: What about PO and GSL because you cannot generate a shipping label without packing and if you pack you generate the label

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: So yes PO and GSL are equivalent What about GSL and D if you generate a label you dispatch and if you dispatch you have a label

Name: Hypothesis Generation - IND
Text: What about RI and PI because if you reserve items then you pick them right

Name: Backtracking - PE
Text: Wait but could you reserve and then cancel before picking Then RI 1 PI 0 So then RI and PI would not be equivalent

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Lets go back to Always Before constraints The normal flow is RO CA RI PI QC PO GSL D IC RP

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: If CA occurs then RO must have occurred before So CA RO is always before RI comes after CA so RI CA PI RI QC PI PO QC GSL PO D GSL IC D RP IC

Name: Hypothesis Generation - IND
Text: Are there indirect always before relations like if PI occurs then RO was before too

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Always After means if the first activity occurs then the second activity is executed in one of the following events

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: So for example RO CA is always after similarly CA RI RI PI PI QC QC PO PO GSL GSL D D IC IC RP

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - IND
Text: Never Together constraints Is there any pair that cannot coexist in the same case Maybe none

Name: Validation - PE
Text: I cannot think of any pair that can never be in the same case so maybe never together is empty

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Directly Follows constraints are the immediate next steps in the happy path

Name: Hypothesis Generation - IND
Text: RO CA CA RI RI PI PI QC QC PO PO GSL GSL D D IC IC RP

Name: Validation - PE
Text: The scenario does not mention loops so maybe it is a straight through process

Name: Backtracking - PE
Text: Wait but if availability check fails then CA does not always lead to RI so CA RI may not be directly follows

Name: Backtracking - PE
Text: Similarly IC RP may not be directly follows because payment may never occur

Name: Hypothesis Generation - IND
Text: Maybe CA is also exactly 1 because every order that comes in is checked for availability

Name: Deductive Reasoning - IND
Text: Then RO and CA would be equivalent because both would always be 1

Name: Hypothesis Generation - IND
Text: Maybe RI PI QC PO GSL D IC all have the same count either all 0 or all 1

Name: Backtracking - PE
Text: Oh right if QC fails then QC can be 1 while PO is 0 so QC and PO are not equivalent

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: So RI PI QC can still have the same count because if RI is 1 then PI and QC are executed and if RI is 0 then both are 0

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: PO GSL D IC can have the same count because if PO occurs then the label dispatch and invoicing occur and if PO does not occur none of them occur

Name: Validation - PE
Text: So equivalence pairs are among RI PI QC and among PO GSL D IC

Name: Backtracking - PE
Text: Wait maybe CA is not always 1 because an order can be canceled right after RO before checking availability

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: That means RO is always 1 but CA can be 0 or 1 so RO and CA are not equivalent

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: The minimum case can be just RO Another case can be RO CA Another can be RO CA RI PI QC and another can be the full process ending with RP

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: So activ freq becomes RO 1 and every other activity 0 or 1

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Equivalence should therefore be RI PI RI QC PI QC and PO GSL PO D PO IC GSL D GSL IC D IC

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Always before can be expressed with immediate predecessors only CA RO RI CA PI RI QC PI PO QC GSL PO D GSL IC D RP IC

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: Including only immediate always before pairs is sufficient and more concise because the transitive ones are implied

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Never together still appears to be empty because no listed activities are mutually exclusive

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Directly follows should include only mandatory immediate successors RO CA RI PI PI QC PO GSL GSL D D IC and should exclude CA RI QC PO and IC RP because those are optional continuations
